subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Master complex balancing including fractional coefficients and multi-step reactions
Analyze thermochemical equations and the energetics of bond breaking/forming
Evaluate decomposition reactions in terms of activation energy (Thermal, Electrolytic, Photolytic)
Predict displacement outcomes using the electrochemical series and concentration factors
Deep dive into Redox: Identifying agents in complex non-oxygen/hydrogen ionic equations
Apply chemical kinetics to explain corrosion rates and rancidity prevention at a molecular level

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Stoichiometry and Balancing
topics_covered: The Mathematics of the Law of Conservation of Mass
Algebraic method vs. Hit and Trial for complex equations
Information-rich equations (catalysts, temperature, pressure)
teaching_strategy: Algebraic balancing tutorial on the board
Case study: Haber’s Process and Photosynthesis equations
Derivation of mass ratios in reactants
student_activities: Calculate the total mass of reactants vs products for a given reaction

lesson_plan.title: Thermodynamics in Reactions
topics_covered: Enthalpy changes: Exothermic vs. Endothermic mechanisms
Decomposition of Ferrous Sulphate: The role of $SO_{2}$ and $SO_{3}$ gases
Thermal decomposition of Calcium Carbonate in industrial contexts
teaching_strategy: Energy level diagrams (Reactants vs. Products)
Comparison of decomposition catalysts
Critical analysis of 'Why' certain bonds break first
student_activities: Sketch energy profile diagrams for respiration and photosynthesis
Research: Why is the decomposition of $AgCl$ used in black and white photography?
assessment: Short essay on the industrial importance of decomposition

lesson_plan.title: Electrolytic and Photolytic Mechanisms
topics_covered: Mechanism of electrolysis of water (Molar ratios of $H_{2}$ and $O_{2}$)
Photolysis of Silver Bromide and Silver Chloride
Relationship between light frequency and decomposition
teaching_strategy: Detailed schematic of an electrolytic cell
Analysis of gas volume ratios ($2:1$ ratio of Hydrogen to Oxygen)
Discussion on storage of chemicals in dark bottles
student_activities: Calculate theoretical gas volumes in electrolysis
Formulate a hypothesis on why $AgBr$ is more light-sensitive than $AgCl$

lesson_plan.title: Displacement Dynamics
topics_covered: Reactivity series: Beyond the basics (K to Au)
Displacement in non-aqueous solutions
Thermite reaction and its engineering applications
teaching_strategy: Analyzing the Thermite reaction ($Fe_{2}O_{3} + Al$)
Predictive modeling: Will 'Metal A' displace 'Metal B' in complex salts?
Video analysis of high-energy displacement
student_activities: Design an experiment to rank 4 unknown metals by reactivity
Write the ionic equations for displacement reactions

lesson_plan.title: Ionic Perspectives of Double Displacement
topics_covered: Precipitation vs. Neutralization reactions
Complete Ionic and Net Ionic equations
Solubility rules (Introduction to why some salts don't precipitate)
teaching_strategy: Step-by-step conversion of molecular equations to net ionic equations
Demonstration of $Pb(NO_{3})_{2} + KI$ (Golden Rain experiment)
Identifying 'Spectator Ions'
student_activities: Write net ionic equations for 5 precipitation reactions
Predict the color and state of products in ion-exchange

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Redox and Industrial Impact
topics_covered: Redox in terms of electron transfer (Oil Rig)
Corrosion: The Electrochemical theory of Rusting
Antioxidants at a molecular level: How they stop free radicals
teaching_strategy: Introduction to Oxidation Numbers (simplified)
Comparison of Galvanization vs. Electroplating
Analyzing the chemistry of 'Self-protecting' metals like Aluminum
student_activities: Identify Redox agents in equations without Oxygen ($Zn + Cu^{2+} \rightarrow Zn^{2+} + Cu$)
Create a flow chart for the prevention of rancidity in different food types

lesson_plan.title: Synthesis, Revision, and Board Excellence
topics_covered: Integration of all reaction types
HOTS (Higher Order Thinking Skills) questions
Assertion-Reason and Case-based passage practice
teaching_strategy: Solving NCERT Exemplar 'Long Answer' questions
Mock Board Exam simulation (focused on 'Hard' difficulty)
Review of common 'Tricky' questions
student_activities: Solve a multi-step 'Identify A, B, and C' chemical puzzle
Participate in a 'Teacher for 5 minutes' session explaining a concept
assessment: Evaluation of logical reasoning in descriptive answers

teaching_aids: NCERT Exemplar and Reference Material (Lakhmir Singh/Manjit Kaur)
Advanced Lab Chemicals (Potassium Iodide, Silver Nitrate)
teacher_tip: At the 'Hard' level, students often get confused between physical observation and chemical theory. Always tie a complex equation back to a visible change (like the 'rotten egg' smell of $H_{2}S$ or the 'pop' sound of $H_{2}$) to keep the chemistry grounded.
